success_surface: Formula debugging and correction for multi-condition lookup tasks
effective_behaviors: Complete workbook structure inspection before modification
Multi-factor problem diagnosis for broken formulas
SUMPRODUCT selection for conditional aggregation across ranges
Preservation of original file through output to new path
evidence: Agent inspected sheet names, dimensions, and cell contents before coding solution
Agent identified three distinct formula issues: missing aggregation, wrong return cell reference, and array multiplication problem
Agent selected SUMPRODUCT over IF for proper boolean array evaluation
Agent verified the updated formula value after saving
Agent saved to output path rather than modifying source directly

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Workbook Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure (sheets, dimensions, key cells) before applying changes
memory_items.generalization: Before modifying any spreadsheet, programmatically load and display sheet names, row/column counts, and relevant cell values to confirm understanding of data layout

memory_items.title: Conditional Aggregation Formula Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Use SUMPRODUCT for multi-condition lookups that require aggregation across matching rows
memory_items.generalization: When tasked with extracting aggregated values based on multiple criteria from a different table, replace IF-based array formulas with SUMPRODUCT using boolean multiplication of condition arrays

memory_items.title: Formula Debugging Methodology
memory_items.lesson: Diagnose all formula failures systematically rather than fixing single visible errors
memory_items.generalization: When repairing a formula, identify structural issues (aggregation logic, cell references, array handling) separately before implementing corrections

memory_items.title: Post-Write Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Verify formula content immediately after writing to target cell
memory_items.generalization: After assigning any formula to a cell, read back and log the value to confirm correct string representation was persisted

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Debugging & Correction
- **Diagnostic Workflow**: When repairing a formula, inspect both the formula structure and referenced cell/row relationships before implementation.
- **Array Aggregation Pattern**: For conditional lookups requiring aggregation across multiple matching rows, prefer SUMPRODUCT($A$range=$criterion1)*($B$range=$criterion2),$Value$range) over IF() with array logic.
- **Reference Validation**: Verify that returned values reference the correct column containing target data, not hardcoded cell addresses.
- **Syntax Verification**: After writing formulas, re-read cell values to confirm the formula string persisted correctly (not evaluated or corrupted).
- **Multi-Criteria Handling**: Boolean multiplication (* between conditions) works within SUMPRODUCT for AND logic across rows.
